1: ---
2: title: "Core Scripts: Context Workflow Modules"
3: description: "TypeScript modules that generate, score, write and index Spec Kit continuity context."
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "core workflow"
6:   - "memory workflow"
7:   - "subfolder resolution"
8:   - "daemon detect"
9: ---
10: 
11: # Core Scripts: Context Workflow Modules
12: 
13: > TypeScript modules for context-save orchestration, scoring, file output, indexing hooks and daemon detection.
14: 
15: ---
16: 
17: ## 1. OVERVIEW
18: 
19: `scripts/core/` contains the TypeScript workflow modules used by `scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js`. The folder owns context-save orchestration, quality scoring, metadata extraction, file writing, indexing hooks, spec-folder path handling and live daemon detection.
20: 
21: Current state:
22: 
23: - Source of truth is `scripts/core/*.ts`.
24: - Compiled runtime output is `scripts/dist/core/*.js`.
25: - `workflow.ts` composes the save flow and imports focused helpers from this folder.
26: - `daemon-detect.ts` decides whether the standalone indexer may open a writer or must defer to the running MCP daemon.
27: 
28: ---
29: 
30: ## 2. ARCHITECTURE
31: 
32: ```text
33: ╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
34: │                         scripts/core/                            │
35: ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
36: 
37: ┌──────────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────────┐
38: │ generate-context.js  │ ───▶ │ workflow.ts          │
39: │ dist/memory caller   │      │ save orchestration   │
40: └──────────────────────┘      └──────────┬───────────┘
41:                                           │
42:                        ┌──────────────────┼──────────────────┐
43:                        ▼                  ▼                  ▼
44:               ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐
45:               │ path + config  │ │ metadata +     │ │ daemon-detect  │
46:               │ config/        │ │ scoring        │ │ writer-safety  │
47:               │ subfolders     │ │ memory/title/  │ │ gate           │
48:               └───────┬────────┘ │ topic/quality  │ └────────────────┘
49:                       │          └───────┬────────┘
50:                       ▼                  ▼
51:               ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐
52:               │ writers +      │ │ post-save      │
53:               │ frontmatter    │ │ review/drift   │
54:               └────────────────┘ └────────────────┘
55: 
56: Dependency direction:
57: workflow.ts -> focused core helpers -> scripts/lib utilities
58: index.ts -> public exports only
59: ```
60: 
61: ---
62: 
63: ## 3. PACKAGE TOPOLOGY
64: 
65: ```text
66: scripts/core/
67: +-- index.ts                  # Public barrel for core modules
68: +-- workflow.ts               # Main context-save orchestration
69: +-- workflow-accessors.ts     # Typed accessors for workflow objects
70: +-- workflow-path-utils.ts    # Path normalization and key-file discovery
71: +-- config.ts                 # Runtime config, constants and specs-dir resolution
72: +-- daemon-detect.ts          # MCP-daemon liveness gate (lease + process probe)
73: +-- subfolder-utils.ts        # Spec folder and child-folder resolution
74: +-- save-context-path.ts      # Save path resolution helpers
75: +-- memory-*.ts               # Metadata and indexing support
76: +-- *-scorer.ts               # Quality scoring support
77: +-- *-validator.ts            # Alignment and quality gate checks
78: `-- README.md
79: ```
80: 
81: Allowed dependency direction:
82: 
83: ```text
84: workflow.ts -> core helper modules -> scripts/lib utilities
85: index.ts -> core helper modules
86: core helper modules -> Node built-ins and local script libraries
87: ```
88: 
89: Disallowed dependency direction:
90: 
91: ```text
92: core helper modules -> scripts/dist compiled output
93: scripts/lib utilities -> workflow.ts
94: runtime callers -> private helper assumptions not exported by index.ts
95: ```
96: 
97: ---
98: 
99: ## 4. DIRECTORY TREE
100: 
101: ```text
102: scripts/core/
103: +-- alignment-validator.ts       # Spec-folder alignment and thinning targets
104: +-- config.ts                    # Config loading, path wiring, specs-dir discovery
105: +-- content-cleaner.ts           # HTML stripping and literal-anchor escaping
106: +-- daemon-detect.ts             # MCP-daemon liveness check before standalone indexing
107: +-- find-predecessor-memory.ts   # Prior memory lookup support
108: +-- frontmatter-editor.ts        # Frontmatter injection and trigger rendering
109: +-- index.ts                     # Barrel exports
110: +-- memory-indexer.ts            # Indexing hooks and metadata preparation
111: +-- memory-metadata.ts           # Memory classification and evidence snapshots
112: +-- post-save-review.ts          # Post-save frontmatter drift review
113: +-- quality-gates.ts             # Save and indexing gate decisions
114: +-- quality-scorer.ts            # Artifact quality scoring
115: +-- save-context-path.ts         # Save target path helpers
116: +-- subfolder-utils.ts           # Spec folder resolution helpers
117: +-- title-builder.ts             # Memory title construction
118: +-- topic-extractor.ts           # Topic signal extraction
119: +-- tree-thinning.ts             # Context tree compaction helpers
120: +-- workflow-accessors.ts        # Safe accessors for loose workflow data
121: +-- workflow-path-utils.ts       # Path normalization and key-file discovery
122: +-- workflow.ts                  # Main orchestration flow
123: `-- README.md
124: ```
125: 
126: ---
127: 
128: ## 5. KEY FILES
129: 
130: | File | Responsibility |
131: |---|---|
132: | `workflow.ts` | Runs the context-save flow from parsed input through generated continuity artifacts; serializes runs with an in-process queue plus filesystem lock and gates Step 11.5 auto-indexing on daemon liveness. |
133: | `config.ts` | Loads `config.jsonc`, validates and normalizes workflow limits, freezes the `CONFIG` object, and resolves the active specs directories. |
134: | `daemon-detect.ts` | Reports whether the `mk-spec-memory` daemon is alive by combining the launcher lease with live process probing, so a standalone save never opens a second SQLite writer. |
135: | `subfolder-utils.ts` | Resolves spec folders, child folders and subfolder-aware save targets. |
136: | `save-context-path.ts` | Computes canonical save paths for generated context output. |
137: | `memory-metadata.ts` | Builds metadata used by memory records, deduplication, causal links and evidence snapshots. |
138: | `memory-indexer.ts` | Prepares indexing calls and memory metadata for saved artifacts. |
139: | `frontmatter-editor.ts` | Injects quality and spec-doc-health metadata and renders trigger-phrase frontmatter. |
140: | `post-save-review.ts` | Compares saved frontmatter with JSON payloads and reports drift findings. |
141: | `quality-gates.ts` | Decides whether save and indexing quality gates pass or abort. |
142: | `index.ts` | Exposes the modules that callers can import from `core`. |
143: 
144: ---
145: 
146: ## 6. BOUNDARIES AND FLOW
147: 
148: | Boundary | Rule |
149: |---|---|
150: | Imports | Source modules import local TypeScript helpers and script libraries, not compiled `dist/` output. `daemon-detect.ts` is stdlib-only (`node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:url`). |
151: | Exports | `index.ts` is the public barrel for this folder. Keep one-off helpers private unless another script imports them. |
152: | Ownership | This folder owns context-save orchestration helpers and the writer-safety daemon gate. MCP server tools, database code and spec templates belong outside `scripts/core/`. |
153: 
154: Daemon-liveness gate:
155: 
156: ```text
157: ╭──────────────────────────────────────────╮
158: │ standalone save reaches Step 11.5        │
159: ╰──────────────────────────────────────────╯
160:                   │
161:                   ▼
162: ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
163: │ readLeasePids(): primaryPid + childPid   │
164: │ from .mk-spec-memory-launcher.json       │
165: └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
166:                   │
167:                   ▼
168: ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
169: │ primaryPid alive?  ──── yes ──▶ ALIVE     │
170: │ else childPid alive? ── yes ──▶ ALIVE     │
171: │ else ───────────────────────▶ NOT ALIVE   │
172: └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
173:                   │
174:                   ▼
175: ╭──────────────────────────────────────────╮
176: │ ALIVE -> skip standalone index (use MCP) │
177: │ NOT ALIVE -> safe to index in-process    │
178: ╰──────────────────────────────────────────╯
179: ```
180: 
181: The childPid branch is load-bearing: a launcher pid that looks dead does not make the lease reclaimable while the recorded child (the real SQLite writer) is still live. Reclaiming there would spawn a second writer on `context-index.sqlite`.
182: 
183: ---
184: 
185: ## 7. ENTRYPOINTS
186: 
187: | Entrypoint | Type | Purpose |
188: |---|---|---|
189: | `runWorkflow` | Function (`workflow.ts`) | Main context-save orchestration entry, serialized under the run lock. |
190: | `isSpecMemoryDaemonAlive` | Function (`daemon-detect.ts`) | Returns `{ alive, pid }`; the writer-safety gate for standalone indexing. |
191: | `isProcessAlive` | Function (`daemon-detect.ts`) | Liveness probe via `process.kill(pid, 0)`, reused by the workflow lock cleanup. |
192: | `resolveSpecMemoryDaemonLeasePath` | Function (`daemon-detect.ts`) | Resolves the launcher lease path under `mcp_server/database/`. |
193: | `CONFIG` / `findActiveSpecsDir` | Export (`config.ts`) | Frozen runtime config and active specs-directory resolution. |
194: | `index.ts` | Module | Public barrel for importing core helpers. |
195: | `scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js` | CLI script | Primary caller for core workflow behavior. |
196: 
197: ---
198: 
199: ## 8. VALIDATION
200: 
201: Run from the repository root unless noted.
202: 
203: ```bash
204: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts run build
205: ```
206: 
207: Expected result: TypeScript compiles and updates `scripts/dist/`.
208: 
209: The sibling Vitest suite lives in `../tests/`. Targeted runs for this folder, from `scripts/`:
210: 
211: ```bash
212: npm test
213: npx vitest run tests/daemon-detect.vitest.ts
214: npx vitest run tests/workflow-step115-daemon-guard.vitest.ts
215: npx vitest run tests/workflow-canonical-save-metadata.vitest.ts
216: ```
217: 
218: Expected result: Vitest reports the daemon-detection, Step-11.5 guard and canonical-save suites passing.
219: 
220: ---
221: 
222: ## 9. RELATED
223: 
224: - [`../README.md`](../README.md)
225: - [`../tests/README.md`](../tests/README.md)
226: - [`../../README.md`](../../README.md)

(End of file - total 226 lines)
</content>

# Core Scripts: Context Workflow Modules

> TypeScript modules for context-save orchestration, scoring, file output, indexing hooks and daemon detection.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

`scripts/core/` contains the TypeScript workflow modules used by `scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js`. The folder owns context-save orchestration, quality scoring, metadata extraction, file writing, indexing hooks, spec-folder path handling and live daemon detection.

Current state:

- Source of truth is `scripts/core/*.ts`.
- Compiled runtime output is `scripts/dist/core/*.js`.
- `workflow.ts` composes the save flow and imports focused helpers from this folder.
- `daemon-detect.ts` decides whether the standalone indexer may open a writer or must defer to the running MCP daemon.

---

## 2. ARCHITECTURE

```text
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                         scripts/core/                            │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

┌──────────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────────┐
│ generate-context.js  │ ───▶ │ workflow.ts          │
│ dist/memory caller   │      │ save orchestration   │
└──────────────────────┘      └──────────┬───────────┘
                                          │
                       ┌──────────────────┼──────────────────┐
                       ▼                  ▼                  ▼
              ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐
              │ path + config  │ │ metadata +     │ │ daemon-detect  │
              │ config/        │ │ scoring        │ │ writer-safety  │
              │ subfolders     │ │ memory/title/  │ │ gate           │
              └───────┬────────┘ │ topic/quality  │ └────────────────┘
                      │          └───────┬────────┘
                      ▼                  ▼
              ┌────────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐
              │ writers +      │ │ post-save      │
              │ frontmatter    │ │ review/drift   │
              └────────────────┘ └────────────────┘

Dependency direction:
workflow.ts -> focused core helpers -> scripts/lib utilities
index.ts -> public exports only
```

---

## 3. PACKAGE TOPOLOGY

```text
scripts/core/
+-- index.ts                  # Public barrel for core modules
+-- workflow.ts               # Main context-save orchestration
+-- workflow-accessors.ts     # Typed accessors for workflow objects
+-- workflow-path-utils.ts    # Path normalization and key-file discovery
+-- config.ts                 # Runtime config, constants and specs-dir resolution
+-- daemon-detect.ts          # MCP-daemon liveness gate (lease + process probe)
+-- subfolder-utils.ts        # Spec folder and child-folder resolution
+-- save-context-path.ts      # Save path resolution helpers
+-- memory-*.ts               # Metadata and indexing support
+-- *-scorer.ts               # Quality scoring support
+-- *-validator.ts            # Alignment and quality gate checks
`-- README.md
```

Allowed dependency direction:

```text
workflow.ts -> core helper modules -> scripts/lib utilities
index.ts -> core helper modules
core helper modules -> Node built-ins and local script libraries
```

Disallowed dependency direction:

```text
core helper modules -> scripts/dist compiled output
scripts/lib utilities -> workflow.ts
runtime callers -> private helper assumptions not exported by index.ts
```

---

## 4. DIRECTORY TREE

```text
scripts/core/
+-- alignment-validator.ts       # Spec-folder alignment and thinning targets
+-- config.ts                    # Config loading, path wiring, specs-dir discovery
+-- content-cleaner.ts           # HTML stripping and literal-anchor escaping
+-- daemon-detect.ts             # MCP-daemon liveness check before standalone indexing
+-- find-predecessor-memory.ts   # Prior memory lookup support
+-- frontmatter-editor.ts        # Frontmatter injection and trigger rendering
+-- index.ts                     # Barrel exports
+-- memory-indexer.ts            # Indexing hooks and metadata preparation
+-- memory-metadata.ts           # Memory classification and evidence snapshots
+-- post-save-review.ts          # Post-save frontmatter drift review
+-- quality-gates.ts             # Save and indexing gate decisions
+-- quality-scorer.ts            # Artifact quality scoring
+-- save-context-path.ts         # Save target path helpers
+-- subfolder-utils.ts           # Spec folder resolution helpers
+-- title-builder.ts             # Memory title construction
+-- topic-extractor.ts           # Topic signal extraction
+-- tree-thinning.ts             # Context tree compaction helpers
+-- workflow-accessors.ts        # Safe accessors for loose workflow data
+-- workflow-path-utils.ts       # Path normalization and key-file discovery
+-- workflow.ts                  # Main orchestration flow
`-- README.md
```

---

## 5. KEY FILES

| File | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `workflow.ts` | Runs the context-save flow from parsed input through generated continuity artifacts; serializes runs with an in-process queue plus filesystem lock and gates Step 11.5 auto-indexing on daemon liveness. |
| `config.ts` | Loads `config.jsonc`, validates and normalizes workflow limits, freezes the `CONFIG` object, and resolves the active specs directories. |
| `daemon-detect.ts` | Reports whether the `mk-spec-memory` daemon is alive by combining the launcher lease with live process probing, so a standalone save never opens a second SQLite writer. |
| `subfolder-utils.ts` | Resolves spec folders, child folders and subfolder-aware save targets. |
| `save-context-path.ts` | Computes canonical save paths for generated context output. |
| `memory-metadata.ts` | Builds metadata used by memory records, deduplication, causal links and evidence snapshots. |
| `memory-indexer.ts` | Prepares indexing calls and memory metadata for saved artifacts. |
| `frontmatter-editor.ts` | Injects quality and spec-doc-health metadata and renders trigger-phrase frontmatter. |
| `post-save-review.ts` | Compares saved frontmatter with JSON payloads and reports drift findings. |
| `quality-gates.ts` | Decides whether save and indexing quality gates pass or abort. |
| `index.ts` | Exposes the modules that callers can import from `core`. |

---

## 6. BOUNDARIES AND FLOW

| Boundary | Rule |
|---|---|
| Imports | Source modules import local TypeScript helpers and script libraries, not compiled `dist/` output. `daemon-detect.ts` is stdlib-only (`node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:url`). |
| Exports | `index.ts` is the public barrel for this folder. Keep one-off helpers private unless another script imports them. |
| Ownership | This folder owns context-save orchestration helpers and the writer-safety daemon gate. MCP server tools, database code and spec templates belong outside `scripts/core/`. |

Daemon-liveness gate:

```text
╭──────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ standalone save reaches Step 11.5        │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────╯
                  │
                  ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ readLeasePids(): primaryPid + childPid   │
│ from .mk-spec-memory-launcher.json       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
                  │
                  ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ primaryPid alive?  ──── yes ──▶ ALIVE     │
│ else childPid alive? ── yes ──▶ ALIVE     │
│ else ───────────────────────▶ NOT ALIVE   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
                  │
                  ▼
╭──────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ALIVE -> skip standalone index (use MCP) │
│ NOT ALIVE -> safe to index in-process    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

The childPid branch is load-bearing: a launcher pid that looks dead does not make the lease reclaimable while the recorded child (the real SQLite writer) is still live. Reclaiming there would spawn a second writer on `context-index.sqlite`.

---

## 7. ENTRYPOINTS

| Entrypoint | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `runWorkflow` | Function (`workflow.ts`) | Main context-save orchestration entry, serialized under the run lock. |
| `isSpecMemoryDaemonAlive` | Function (`daemon-detect.ts`) | Returns `{ alive, pid }`; the writer-safety gate for standalone indexing. |
| `isProcessAlive` | Function (`daemon-detect.ts`) | Liveness probe via `process.kill(pid, 0)`, reused by the workflow lock cleanup. |
| `resolveSpecMemoryDaemonLeasePath` | Function (`daemon-detect.ts`) | Resolves the launcher lease path under `mcp_server/database/`. |
| `CONFIG` / `findActiveSpecsDir` | Export (`config.ts`) | Frozen runtime config and active specs-directory resolution. |
| `index.ts` | Module | Public barrel for importing core helpers. |
| `scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js` | CLI script | Primary caller for core workflow behavior. |

---

## 8. VALIDATION

Run from the repository root unless noted.

```bash
npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts run build
```

Expected result: TypeScript compiles and updates `scripts/dist/`.

The sibling Vitest suite lives in `../tests/`. Targeted runs for this folder, from `scripts/`:

```bash
npm test
npx vitest run tests/daemon-detect.vitest.ts
npx vitest run tests/workflow-step115-daemon-guard.vitest.ts
npx vitest run tests/workflow-canonical-save-metadata.vitest.ts
```

Expected result: Vitest reports the daemon-detection, Step-11.5 guard and canonical-save suites passing.

---

## 9. RELATED

- [`../README.md`](../README.md)
- [`../tests/README.md`](../tests/README.md)
- [`../../README.md`](../../README.md)

# Ops Self-Healing Scripts

## 1. OVERVIEW

`scripts/ops/` contains deterministic shell runbooks for known spec-kit operational failure classes. Each healer runs detect, repair, and verify steps with bounded retry behavior and emits a structured escalation payload when retry budget is exhausted.

## 2. SCRIPT IO

| Flow | Input | Output |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Class listing | `runbook.sh list` | Supported failure class keys |
| Runbook detail | `runbook.sh show <class>` | Human-readable runbook for one class |
| Drill execution | `runbook.sh drill <class|all> --scenario <success|escalate>` | Success output or escalation JSON |
| Healer execution | Failure class plus retry options | Deterministic detect, repair, and verify sequence |

Supported classes are `index-drift`, `session-ambiguity`, `ledger-mismatch`, and `telemetry-drift`.

## 3. ENTRYPOINTS

- `runbook.sh list` prints supported failure classes.
- `runbook.sh show <class>` prints one class runbook.
- `runbook.sh drill <class|all> --scenario <success|escalate> --max-attempts <n>` runs bounded remediation drills.
- `heal-*.sh` scripts run class-specific detect, repair, and verify flows.
- `ops-common.sh` provides shared retry, logging, and escalation helpers.
- `process-memory-harness.ts` captures process/RSS/swap/wired snapshots used by arc 009 memory evidence.
- `process-sweep.ts` emits non-destructive termination plans from exact ownership evidence; no live apply command exists.

## 4. VALIDATION FROM REPO ROOT

Run ops validation from the repository root:

```bash
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/ops/runbook.sh list
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/ops/runbook.sh show index-drift
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/ops/runbook.sh drill all --scenario success --max-attempts 1
python3 .opencode/skills/sk-code/assets/scripts/verify_alignment_drift.py --root .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/ops
```

## 5. KEY FILES

| File | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `ops-common.sh` | Shared retry, logging, option parsing, and escalation helpers |
| `heal-index-drift.sh` | Remediation workflow for index drift failures |
| `heal-session-ambiguity.sh` | Remediation workflow for session ambiguity failures |
| `heal-ledger-mismatch.sh` | Remediation workflow for ledger mismatch failures |
| `heal-telemetry-drift.sh` | Remediation workflow for telemetry drift failures |
| `runbook.sh` | Class listing, runbook display, and drill orchestration |
| `process-memory-harness.ts` | Process inventory, memory snapshot and exact-identity classification helper |
| `process-sweep.ts` | Dry-run sweep planner; emits `eligibleForTermination` evidence but never sends signals |

Arc 009 lifecycle helper map:

| Surface | Helper |
| --- | --- |
| Deep loop runtime | `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/loop-lock.ts`, `jsonl-repair.ts`, `atomic-state.ts` |
| Code Graph | `system-code-graph/mcp_server/lib/owner-lease.ts`, `canonical-db-dir.ts`, `close-db-assertion.ts` |
| Spec Kit runtime | `mcp_server/lib/memory/bounded-cache.ts`, `audit-rotation.ts`, `mcp_server/lib/runtime/timer-registry.ts`, `shutdown-hooks.ts`, `mcp_server/lib/embedders/sidecar-client.ts` |
| Ops | `scripts/ops/process-memory-harness.ts`, `scripts/ops/process-sweep.ts` |

## 6. BOUNDARIES

- Ops scripts model known failure classes; they are not a general incident-management system.
- Healers must keep bounded retries and emit one escalation JSON line on retry exhaustion.
- Scripts should remain deterministic so drills and release gates are repeatable.

## 7. RELATED

- `../README.md`
- `../spec/README.md`
- `../../mcp_server/README.md`

10: # Setup Scripts
11: 
12: > Shell and Node.js utilities for checking spec-folder readiness and local runtime prerequisites.
13: 
14: ---
15: 
16: ## 1. OVERVIEW
17: 
18: `scripts/setup/` contains preflight utilities for Spec Kit workflows. These scripts check whether a target spec folder has the required documents, whether native Node.js modules are loadable, and whether the recorded Node.js runtime has changed.
19: 
20: Current state:
21: 
22: - Shell entrypoints are run directly from source.
23: - Node.js helpers run from the scripts workspace.
24: - Full spec-folder validation delegates to `scripts/spec/validate.sh` when requested.
25: 
26: ---
27: 
28: ## 2. PACKAGE TOPOLOGY
29: 
30: ```text
31: scripts/setup/
32: +-- check-prerequisites.sh       # Spec-folder readiness checks
33: +-- check-native-modules.sh      # Native module availability checks
34: +-- rebuild-native-modules.sh    # Native module rebuild helper
35: +-- record-node-version.js       # Node.js version recorder
36: `-- README.md
37: ```
38: 
39: Allowed direction:
40: 
41: - Setup scripts may call `scripts/spec/validate.sh` for strict spec-folder validation.
42: - Native-module scripts may inspect package-local dependencies.
43: - Callers may parse `--json` or `--paths-only` output from `check-prerequisites.sh`.
44: 
45: Disallowed direction:
46: 
47: - Setup scripts should not create or edit spec documents.
48: - Setup scripts should not import generated `dist/` modules unless a command explicitly checks built output.
49: - Runtime callers should not depend on human-readable output when a machine flag exists.
50: 
51: ---
52: 
53: ## 3. KEY FILES
54: 
55: | File | Responsibility |
56: |---|---|
57: | `check-prerequisites.sh` | Checks spec-folder presence, required docs, optional task docs and validation status. |
58: | `check-native-modules.sh` | Verifies native modules required by the scripts workspace can load. |
59: | `rebuild-native-modules.sh` | Rebuilds native modules after Node.js or dependency changes. |
60: | `record-node-version.js` | Records the active Node.js version for rebuild checks. |
61: 
62: ---
63: 
64: ## 4. COMMANDS
65: 
66: Run from the repository root unless noted.
67: 
68: ```bash
69: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-prerequisites.sh
70: ```
71: 
72: Expected result: prints `FEATURE_DIR` and available supporting documents for the active spec folder.
73: 
74: ```bash
75: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-prerequisites.sh --json --validate-strict
76: ```
77: 
78: Expected result: emits JSON and exits non-zero if strict spec validation fails.
79: 
80: ```bash
81: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-native-modules.sh
82: ```
83: 
84: Expected result: exits zero when native dependencies load in the current Node.js runtime.
85: 
86: ```bash
87: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/rebuild-native-modules.sh
88: ```
89: 
90: Expected result: rebuilds native modules for the current Node.js runtime.
91: 
92: ---
93: 
94: ## 5. BOUNDARIES
95: 
96: | Boundary | Rule |
97: |---|---|
98: | Scope | These scripts report readiness. They do not repair spec folders or choose implementation work. |
99: | Output | Use `--json` for automation and default output for terminal checks. |
100: | Validation | `--validate` and `--validate-strict` delegate to the spec validator instead of duplicating its rules. |
101: | Dependencies | Native-module checks stay limited to package health and rebuild guidance. |
102: 
103: ---
104: 
105: ## 6. VALIDATION
106: 
107: Run the README validator after editing this file:
108: 
109: ```bash
110: python3 .opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/validate_document.py .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/README.md
111: ```
112: 
113: Run command checks after changing setup scripts:

117: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-native-modules.sh
118: ```
119: 
120: Expected result: each command exits zero in a configured workspace.
121:

- Setup scripts may call `scripts/spec/validate.sh` for strict spec-folder validation.
- Native-module scripts may inspect package-local dependencies.
- Callers may parse `--json` or `--paths-only` output from `check-prerequisites.sh`.

Disallowed direction:

- Setup scripts should not create or edit spec documents.
- Setup scripts should not import generated `dist/` modules unless a command explicitly checks built output.
- Runtime callers should not depend on human-readable output when a machine flag exists.

---

## 3. KEY FILES

| File | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `check-prerequisites.sh` | Checks spec-folder presence, required docs, optional task docs and validation status. |
| `check-native-modules.sh` | Verifies native modules required by the scripts workspace can load. |
| `rebuild-native-modules.sh` | Rebuilds native modules after Node.js or dependency changes. |
| `record-node-version.js` | Records the active Node.js version for rebuild checks. |

---

## 4. COMMANDS

Run from the repository root unless noted.

```bash
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-prerequisites.sh
```

Expected result: prints `FEATURE_DIR` and available supporting documents for the active spec folder.

```bash
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-prerequisites.sh --json --validate-strict
```

Expected result: emits JSON and exits non-zero if strict spec validation fails.

```bash
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-native-modules.sh
```

Expected result: exits zero when native dependencies load in the current Node.js runtime.

```bash
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/rebuild-native-modules.sh
```

Expected result: rebuilds native modules for the current Node.js runtime.

---

## 5. BOUNDARIES

| Boundary | Rule |
|---|---|
| Scope | These scripts report readiness. They do not repair spec folders or choose implementation work. |
| Output | Use `--json` for automation and default output for terminal checks. |
| Validation | `--validate` and `--validate-strict` delegate to the spec validator instead of duplicating its rules. |
| Dependencies | Native-module checks stay limited to package health and rebuild guidance. |

---

## 6. VALIDATION

Run the README validator after editing this file:

```bash
python3 .opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/validate_document.py .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/README.md
```

Run command checks after changing setup scripts:

```bash
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-prerequisites.sh --json
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/check-native-modules.sh
```

Expected result: each command exits zero in a configured workspace.

---

## 7. RELATED

- [`../README.md`](../README.md)
- [`../spec/README.md`](../spec/README.md)
- [`../rules/README.md`](../rules/README.md)
- [`../../README.md`](../../README.md)

# Spec Scripts

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

`scripts/spec/` owns shell entrypoints for spec folder lifecycle work. It creates packet folders, upgrades documentation levels, validates structure, checks completion state and archives finished or stale folders.

Current state:

- Shell scripts are the public command surface for spec lifecycle operations.
- Validation delegates rule checks to `../rules/` and shared helpers in `../lib/`.
- Scripts accept explicit spec folder paths and are intended to run from the repository root.

---

## 2. ARCHITECTURE

```text
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                         SPEC SCRIPTS                         │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

┌──────────────┐      ┌────────────────┐      ┌────────────────┐
│ Operator     │ ───▶ │ create.sh      │ ───▶ │ Spec folder    │
│ or command   │      │ upgrade-level  │      │ files          │
└──────┬───────┘      └───────┬────────┘      └───────┬────────┘
       │                      │                       │
       │                      ▼                       ▼
       │              ┌──────────────┐       ┌────────────────┐
       └────────────▶ │ validate.sh  │ ───▶  │ rules/check-*  │
                      └──────┬───────┘       └───────┬────────┘
                             │                       │
                             ▼                       ▼
                      ┌──────────────┐       ┌────────────────┐
                      │ completion   │       │ lib/*.sh       │
                      │ and archive  │       │ helpers        │
                      └──────────────┘       └────────────────┘

Dependency direction: spec/*.sh ───▶ rules/*.sh ───▶ lib/*.sh
```

---

## 3. PACKAGE TOPOLOGY

```text
scripts/spec/
+-- create.sh                    # Scaffold spec folders from templates
+-- upgrade-level.sh             # Add level-owned docs and sections
+-- check-placeholders.sh        # Detect unresolved template placeholders
+-- validate.sh                  # Run structural validation rules
+-- progressive-validate.sh      # Staged validation helper
+-- check-completion.sh          # Verify completion checklist state
+-- calculate-completeness.sh    # Report checklist completion metrics
+-- recommend-level.sh           # Recommend documentation level from task signals
+-- archive.sh                   # Move completed or stale spec folders
+-- check-template-staleness.sh  # Compare generated docs with templates
+-- quality-audit.sh             # Batch quality audit helper
`-- README.md
```

Allowed direction:

- `spec/*.sh` may source shared shell helpers from `../lib/`.
- `validate.sh` may call validation rules from `../rules/`.
- Lifecycle scripts may read templates and write only the selected spec folder.

Disallowed direction:

- Rule scripts should not mutate spec content.
- Shell helpers should not call spec lifecycle entrypoints.
- Spec scripts should not depend on generated `dist/` output.

---

## 4. KEY FILES

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `create.sh` | Creates new Level 1 or phase folders from templates. |
| `upgrade-level.sh` | Adds missing files and sections for higher documentation levels. |
| `validate.sh` | Runs the modular validation gate used before completion claims. |
| `check-completion.sh` | Confirms checklist evidence before a task is called complete. |
| `progressive-validate.sh` | Runs a staged validation pass for detect, fix, suggest and report flows. |
| `archive.sh` | Moves completed or stale spec folders into the archive area. |

---

## 5. BOUNDARIES AND FLOW

Main validation flow:

```text
╭──────────────────────────────╮
│ Repository-root command      │
╰──────────────────────────────╯
              │
              ▼
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ scripts/spec/validate.sh     │
└──────────────┬───────────────┘
               ▼
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Load lib shell helpers       │
└──────────────┬───────────────┘
               ▼
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Run rules/check-*.sh         │
└──────────────┬───────────────┘
               ▼
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Print pass, warning or error │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

This folder owns shell orchestration only. Template content belongs under `templates/`, validation rule behavior belongs under `rules/` and shared shell primitives belong under `lib/`.

---

## 6. ENTRYPOINTS

```bash
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/create.sh specs/<name>
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/upgrade-level.sh specs/<name> --to 3
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/check-placeholders.sh specs/<name>
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh specs/<name> --strict
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/check-completion.sh specs/<name>
```

---

## 7. VALIDATION

Use repository-root commands:

```bash
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh specs/<name> --strict
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/check-completion.sh specs/<name>
```

Use `--recursive` with `validate.sh` when the target is a phase parent with child phase folders.

---

## 8. RELATED

- [`../README.md`](../README.md)
- [`../lib/README.md`](../lib/README.md)
- [`../rules/README.md`](../rules/README.md)
- [`../templates/README.md`](../templates/README.md)

# Script Tests

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

`scripts/tests/` validates script behavior across shell workflows, JavaScript
smoke tests, Python checks and Vitest regression suites. It covers validation
orchestration, memory pipeline seams, phase workflows, import policy, template
rendering and utility contracts.

Tests assume the resume contract where packet context is recovered from
`handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs`; generated memory artifacts
remain supporting fixtures or outputs.

---

## 2. PACKAGE TOPOLOGY

```text
scripts/tests/
+-- test-*.js                 # Node smoke and integration tests
+-- test-*.sh                 # Shell validation and phase workflow tests
+-- test_dual_threshold.py    # Python threshold check
+-- *.vitest.ts               # TypeScript regression suites
+-- fixtures/                 # Test-local fixtures
+-- test-fixtures -> ../test-fixtures
`-- README.md
```

Suite groups:

| Group | Coverage |
| --- | --- |
| Shell tests | Spec validation, phase workflow and upgrade-level behavior |
| Node tests | Script modules, extractors, loaders, templates and regressions |
| Vitest suites | Memory, validation, import policy and templates |
| Python tests | Dual-threshold decision behavior |

---

## 3. FIXTURE BOUNDARIES

Allowed fixture content:

- `fixtures/` for test-local inputs and expected outputs.
- `test-fixtures` symlink for shared spec folder validation cases.
- Generated JS artifacts only when intentionally retained.

Not owned here:

- Validation scenario definitions live in `../test-fixtures/`.
- Production templates live under `../../templates/`.
- MCP server fixture JSON lives under `../../mcp_server/tests/fixtures/`.

---

## 4. ENTRYPOINTS

Run from the repository root unless a command changes into `scripts/` explicitly:

```bash
npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts run build
node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test-scripts-modules.js
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test-phase-system.sh
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test-validation.sh
npx --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts vitest run \
  tests/test-integration.vitest.ts
python3 .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test_dual_threshold.py
```

---

## 5. VALIDATION

Recommended targeted order after script changes:

```bash
npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts run build
node .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test-scripts-modules.js
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test-phase-system.sh
bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/test-validation.sh
npx --prefix .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts vitest run \
  tests/test-integration.vitest.ts
```

Use narrower Vitest files for focused changes, then run the shell validation
suite when spec folder behavior changes.

---

## 6. RELATED

- [`../README.md`](../README.md)

- [`../spec/README.md`](../spec/README.md)

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Rebuild every MCP server's dist after a source change, optionally recycle the
3: # transparently-recyclable daemon, and flag launcher changes that a recycle
4: # cannot pick up.
5: #
6: # Why this exists: dist/ is gitignored (a build artifact), so pulling source
7: # changes does NOT update the running servers — each server's dist must be
8: # rebuilt locally. The three MCP servers also build from different roots
9: # (code-graph builds from its SKILL root, not its mcp_server dir), which is
10: # easy to miss and leaves one server running stale code. This script builds
11: # all of them so none is forgotten.
12: #
13: # Recycle model (see references/memory/ for detail):
14: #   - mk-spec-memory recycles transparently: SIGTERM its daemon CHILD and the
15: #     owner launcher respawns it from the fresh dist while the front-proxy keeps
16: #     MCP up. This script does that when --recycle is passed.
17: #   - mk-code-index and mk-skill-advisor launchers EXIT on child SIGTERM instead
18: #     of respawning, so this script never SIGTERMs them; their fresh dist loads
19: #     when they next start.
20: #   - A launcher *.cjs* change never reloads in place (SIGHUP is a shutdown
21: #     signal, not a reload): the running owner launcher holds the old .cjs in
22: #     memory. Such a change goes live only in a FRESH session (a new launcher
23: #     process). This script detects recently-changed launcher .cjs and warns.
24: #
25: # Usage:
26: #   deploy-mcp.sh            # build all dists + report (safe, no recycle)
27: #   deploy-mcp.sh --recycle  # also transparently recycle the mk-spec-memory daemon
28: 
29: set -euo pipefail
30: REPO="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)"
31: cd "$REPO" || exit 1
32: RECYCLE=0; [ "${1:-}" = "--recycle" ] && RECYCLE=1
33: FAIL=0
34: 
35: build_pkg() {
36:   # $1 = human label, $2 = dir to run `npm run build` in
37:   local label="$1" dir="$2"
38:   if [ ! -d "$dir" ]; then echo "  $label: dir missing ($dir) — skipped"; return; fi
39:   # Read package.json via fs (a bare relative path passed to require() resolves
40:   # as a module name, not a file, and would silently report "no build script").
41:   if ! node -e "const p=JSON.parse(require('node:fs').readFileSync('$dir/package.json','utf8'));process.exit(p.scripts&&p.scripts.build?0:1)" 2>/dev/null; then
42:     echo "  $label: no build script — skipped"; return
43:   fi
44:   if ( cd "$dir" && npm run build >/tmp/deploy-mcp-$label.log 2>&1 ); then
45:     echo "  $label: built OK"
46:   else
47:     echo "  $label: BUILD FAILED (see /tmp/deploy-mcp-$label.log)"; FAIL=1
48:   fi
49: }
50: 
51: echo "== Building MCP server dists =="
52: # mk-spec-memory: builds @spec-kit/shared too via TS project references.
53: build_pkg "mk-spec-memory" ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server"
54: # mk-code-index (code-graph): builds from the SKILL root (tsconfig lives there).
55: build_pkg "code-graph" ".opencode/skills/system-code-graph"
56: # mk-skill-advisor: build if it ships a build script.
57: build_pkg "advisor" ".opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server"
58: 
59: if [ "$FAIL" -ne 0 ]; then
60:   echo "!! One or more builds failed — NOT recycling. Fix builds first." >&2
61:   exit 1
62: fi
63: 
64: echo "== Launcher .cjs change check =="
65: # Surface launcher .cjs touched in the working tree or last commit: these need a
66: # FRESH session to take effect, which a recycle cannot provide.
67: CJS_CHANGED="$(
68:   { git diff --name-only -- '.opencode/bin/*.cjs' '.opencode/bin/lib/*.cjs' 2>/dev/null
69:     git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD -- '.opencode/bin/*.cjs' '.opencode/bin/lib/*.cjs' 2>/dev/null
70:   } | sort -u || true
71: )"
72: if [ -n "$CJS_CHANGED" ]; then
73:   echo "  WARNING: launcher .cjs changed — recycle CANNOT activate these."
74:   echo "  Start a FRESH session (new launcher process) to load them:"
75:   echo "$CJS_CHANGED" | sed 's/^/    - /'
76: else
77:   echo "  No launcher .cjs changes detected."
78: fi
79: 
80: if [ "$RECYCLE" -eq 1 ]; then
81:   echo "== Recycling mk-spec-memory daemon (transparent) =="
82:   CHILD="$(pgrep -f 'system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js' | head -1 || true)"
83:   if [ -z "$CHILD" ]; then
84:     echo "  No running mk-spec-memory daemon child found — it will load fresh dist on next start."
85:   else
86:     kill -TERM "$CHILD" && echo "  SIGTERM sent to daemon child $CHILD; owner launcher will respawn from fresh dist."
87:     i=0
88:     while [ $i -lt 60 ]; do
89:       NEW="$(pgrep -f 'system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js' | grep -v "^$CHILD$" | head -1 || true)"
90:       [ -n "$NEW" ] && { echo "  Respawned daemon child: $NEW"; break; }
91:       sleep 0.5; i=$((i+1))
92:     done
93:     [ -z "${NEW:-}" ] && { echo "  !! No respawn within ~30s — check MCP health." >&2; exit 1; }
94:   fi
95:   echo "  Note: code-graph and advisor are not SIGTERM-recycled (their launchers exit, not respawn); fresh dist loads on next start."
96: fi
97: 
98: echo "== Done =="

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Rebuild every MCP server's dist after a source change, optionally recycle the
# transparently-recyclable daemon, and flag launcher changes that a recycle
# cannot pick up.
#
# Why this exists: dist/ is gitignored (a build artifact), so pulling source
# changes does NOT update the running servers — each server's dist must be
# rebuilt locally. The three MCP servers also build from different roots
# (code-graph builds from its SKILL root, not its mcp_server dir), which is
# easy to miss and leaves one server running stale code. This script builds
# all of them so none is forgotten.
#
# Recycle model (see references/memory/ for detail):
#   - mk-spec-memory recycles transparently: SIGTERM its daemon CHILD and the
#     owner launcher respawns it from the fresh dist while the front-proxy keeps
#     MCP up. This script does that when --recycle is passed.
#   - mk-code-index and mk-skill-advisor launchers EXIT on child SIGTERM instead
#     of respawning, so this script never SIGTERMs them; their fresh dist loads
#     when they next start.
#   - A launcher *.cjs* change never reloads in place (SIGHUP is a shutdown
#     signal, not a reload): the running owner launcher holds the old .cjs in
#     memory. Such a change goes live only in a FRESH session (a new launcher
#     process). This script detects recently-changed launcher .cjs and warns.
#
# Usage:
#   deploy-mcp.sh            # build all dists + report (safe, no recycle)
#   deploy-mcp.sh --recycle  # also transparently recycle the mk-spec-memory daemon

set -euo pipefail
REPO="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)"
cd "$REPO" || exit 1
RECYCLE=0; [ "${1:-}" = "--recycle" ] && RECYCLE=1
FAIL=0

build_pkg() {
  # $1 = human label, $2 = dir to run `npm run build` in
  local label="$1" dir="$2"
  if [ ! -d "$dir" ]; then echo "  $label: dir missing ($dir) — skipped"; return; fi
  # Read package.json via fs (a bare relative path passed to require() resolves
  # as a module name, not a file, and would silently report "no build script").
  if ! node -e "const p=JSON.parse(require('node:fs').readFileSync('$dir/package.json','utf8'));process.exit(p.scripts&&p.scripts.build?0:1)" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  $label: no build script — skipped"; return
  fi
  if ( cd "$dir" && npm run build >/tmp/deploy-mcp-$label.log 2>&1 ); then
    echo "  $label: built OK"
  else
    echo "  $label: BUILD FAILED (see /tmp/deploy-mcp-$label.log)"; FAIL=1
  fi
}

echo "== Building MCP server dists =="
# mk-spec-memory: builds @spec-kit/shared too via TS project references.
build_pkg "mk-spec-memory" ".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server"
# mk-code-index (code-graph): builds from the SKILL root (tsconfig lives there).
build_pkg "code-graph" ".opencode/skills/system-code-graph"
# mk-skill-advisor: build if it ships a build script.
build_pkg "advisor" ".opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server"

if [ "$FAIL" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "!! One or more builds failed — NOT recycling. Fix builds first." >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "== Launcher .cjs change check =="
# Surface launcher .cjs touched in the working tree or last commit: these need a
# FRESH session to take effect, which a recycle cannot provide.
CJS_CHANGED="$(
  { git diff --name-only -- '.opencode/bin/*.cjs' '.opencode/bin/lib/*.cjs' 2>/dev/null
    git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD -- '.opencode/bin/*.cjs' '.opencode/bin/lib/*.cjs' 2>/dev/null
  } | sort -u || true
)"
if [ -n "$CJS_CHANGED" ]; then
  echo "  WARNING: launcher .cjs changed — recycle CANNOT activate these."
  echo "  Start a FRESH session (new launcher process) to load them:"
  echo "$CJS_CHANGED" | sed 's/^/    - /'
else
  echo "  No launcher .cjs changes detected."
fi

if [ "$RECYCLE" -eq 1 ]; then
  echo "== Recycling mk-spec-memory daemon (transparent) =="
  CHILD="$(pgrep -f 'system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js' | head -1 || true)"
  if [ -z "$CHILD" ]; then
    echo "  No running mk-spec-memory daemon child found — it will load fresh dist on next start."
  else
    kill -TERM "$CHILD" && echo "  SIGTERM sent to daemon child $CHILD; owner launcher will respawn from fresh dist."
    i=0
    while [ $i -lt 60 ]; do
      NEW="$(pgrep -f 'system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/context-server.js' | grep -v "^$CHILD$" | head -1 || true)"
      [ -n "$NEW" ] && { echo "  Respawned daemon child: $NEW"; break; }
      sleep 0.5; i=$((i+1))
    done
    [ -z "${NEW:-}" ] && { echo "  !! No respawn within ~30s — check MCP health." >&2; exit 1; }
  fi
  echo "  Note: code-graph and advisor are not SIGTERM-recycled (their launchers exit, not respawn); fresh dist loads on next start."
fi

echo "== Done =="

{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Spec Kit Script Registry - Centralized catalog of all scripts for dynamic discovery",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-31",

      "essential": true,
      "inputs": {
        "mode1": "JSON data file path (e.g., /tmp/save-context-data-<session-id>.json)",
        "mode2": "Spec folder path (e.g., 007-feature/)"

      "outputs": ["memory/*.md", "metadata.json"],
      "gate": "Memory Save Rule",

      "dependencies": []
    },

      "calledBy": ["AI Assistant", "Developer"],
      "essential": true,
      "inputs": {

      },
      "outputs": ["Upgraded spec docs", "Backup directory", "JSON result with --json"],
      "gate": "Documentation Level Upgrade",
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "dependencies": ["scripts/lib/shell-common.sh", "templates/"]
    },
    {
      "name": "check-prerequisites",
      "path": "scripts/setup/check-prerequisites.sh",

      "description": "Validate spec folder exists and contains required files for its level",
      "trigger": ["check prerequisites", "verify files"],
      "calledBy": ["AI Assistant", "Implementation Phase"],
      "essential": true,
      "inputs": {
        "options": "--json, --require-tasks, --include-tasks, --paths-only"
      },
      "outputs": ["FEATURE_DIR path", "AVAILABLE_DOCS list"],
      "gate": null,
      "version": "2.0.0",
      "dependencies": []
    },
    {

      "calledBy": ["AI Assistant", "Progress Tracking"],
      "essential": false,
      "inputs": {
        "required": "Spec folder path",
        "options": "--verbose, --json, --file <path>, --quality"
      },
      "outputs": ["Completeness percentage", "Per-file breakdown (with --verbose)"],
      "gate": null,
      "version": "1.0.0",
      "dependencies": []
    },
    {

    {

      "inputs": {
        "options": "--loc <number>, --files <number>, --auth, --api, --db, --architectural, --json"

      "outputs": ["Recommended level (0-3)", "Score breakdown"],

    {

        "required": "Spec folder path",

<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/daemon-detect.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Daemon Detect
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6: // 1. IMPORTS
7: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8: 
9: import * as fs from 'node:fs';
10: import * as path from 'node:path';
11: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
12: 
13: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: // 2. TYPE DEFINITIONS
15: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16: 
17: export interface SpecMemoryDaemonStatus {
18:   readonly alive: boolean;
19:   readonly pid?: number;
20: }
21: 
22: interface LauncherLease {
23:   readonly pid?: unknown;
24:   readonly ownerPid?: unknown;
25:   // The launcher records the spawned daemon (the real SQLite writer) as `childPid`.
26:   // A stale-LOOKING lease (dead launcher pid) whose childPid is still LIVE must NOT be treated
27:   // as reclaimable — the writer is still up. Mirrors the launcher's own liveness rule.
28:   readonly childPid?: unknown;
29: }
30: 
31: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32: // 3. CONSTANTS
33: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34: 
35: const MODULE_DIR = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
36: const LAUNCHER_LEASE_FILE_NAME = '.mk-spec-memory-launcher.json';
37: 
38: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
39: // 4. HELPERS
40: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
41: 
42: function canonicalizePath(filePath: string): string {
43:   const resolved = path.resolve(filePath);
44:   try {
45:     return fs.realpathSync.native(resolved);
46:   } catch {
47:     return resolved;
48:   }
49: }
50: 
51: function positiveInteger(value: unknown): number | null {
52:   return Number.isInteger(value) && Number(value) > 0 ? Number(value) : null;
53: }
54: 
55: interface LeasePids {
56:   /** Primary launcher pid (or legacy ownerPid alias) — reported as status.pid on the common path. */
57:   readonly primaryPid: number | null;
58:   /** Spawned daemon pid (the real SQLite writer), when recorded by the launcher. */
59:   readonly childPid: number | null;
60: }
61: 
62: function readLeasePids(leasePath: string): LeasePids {
63:   try {
64:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(leasePath, 'utf8')) as LauncherLease;
65:     return {
66:       primaryPid: positiveInteger(parsed.pid) ?? positiveInteger(parsed.ownerPid),
67:       childPid: positiveInteger(parsed.childPid),
68:     };
69:   } catch {
70:     return { primaryPid: null, childPid: null };
71:   }
72: }
73: 
74: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75: // 5. CORE LOGIC
76: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77: 
78: export function isProcessAlive(pid: number): boolean {
79:   if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) {
80:     return false;
81:   }
82: 
83:   try {
84:     process.kill(pid, 0);
85:     return true;
86:   } catch (err: unknown) {
87:     const code = (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException)?.code;
88:     return code !== 'ESRCH';
89:   }
90: }
91: 
92: // Walk up to the `system-spec-kit` root rather than a fixed up-count: this module compiles to
93: // scripts/dist/core (3 ups) but could run from scripts/core under tsx (2 ups), so a hardcoded count is
94: // layout-fragile. The launcher writes the lease to <system-spec-kit>/mcp_server/database/ — mirror that.
95: function findSystemSpecKitRoot(startDir: string): string | null {
96:   let dir = startDir;
97:   // eslint-disable-next-line no-constant-condition
98:   while (true) {
99:     if (path.basename(dir) === 'system-spec-kit') return dir;
100:     const parent = path.dirname(dir);
101:     if (parent === dir) return null;
102:     dir = parent;
103:   }
104: }
105: 
106: export function resolveSpecMemoryDaemonLeasePath(): string {
107:   const kitRoot = findSystemSpecKitRoot(MODULE_DIR);
108:   const threeUp = path.resolve(MODULE_DIR, '..', '..', '..', 'mcp_server', 'database');
109:   const dbDir = canonicalizePath(
110:     kitRoot
111:       ? path.join(kitRoot, 'mcp_server', 'database')
112:       // Defensive fallback if the marker is absent: prefer the dist/core (3-up) layout, else 2-up.
113:       : (fs.existsSync(threeUp) ? threeUp : path.resolve(MODULE_DIR, '..', '..', 'mcp_server', 'database')),
114:   );
115:   return path.join(dbDir, LAUNCHER_LEASE_FILE_NAME);
116: }
117: 
118: export function isSpecMemoryDaemonAlive(
119:   leasePath: string = resolveSpecMemoryDaemonLeasePath(),
120: ): SpecMemoryDaemonStatus {
121:   const { primaryPid, childPid } = readLeasePids(leasePath);
122: 
123:   // The lease is reclaimable only when NEITHER the launcher pid NOR its recorded
124:   // childPid (the real writer) is live. Probe the primary first so status.pid stays the
125:   // launcher pid on the common path; if the launcher pid is dead/absent but the recorded
126:   // childPid is still LIVE, the daemon is up — report alive (pinned to childPid) so the
127:   // standalone-save Step-11.5 path does NOT open a second writer on context-index.sqlite.
128:   if (primaryPid !== null && isProcessAlive(primaryPid)) {
129:     return { alive: true, pid: primaryPid };
130:   }
131:   if (childPid !== null && isProcessAlive(childPid)) {
132:     return { alive: true, pid: childPid };
133:   }
134:   return { alive: false };
135: }

(End of file - total 135 lines)
</content>

113: // Trigger phrase filter — suppresses path fragments, short tokens, and shingle subsets.
114: const TRIGGER_ALLOW_LIST = new Set(['rag', 'bm25', 'mcp', 'adr', 'jwt', 'api', 'cli', 'llm', 'ai']);
115: 
116: function normalizeWorkflowTriggerKey(value: string): string {
117:   return value.toLowerCase().replace(/[-_]+/g, ' ').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
118: }
119: 
120: function filterTriggerPhrases(
121:   phrases: string[],
122:   manualPhraseKeys: Set<string> = new Set(),
123: ): string[] {
124:   // Stage 1: Remove entries containing path separators (forward/backslash, multi-word path segments)
125:   let filtered = phrases.filter(p => {
126:     const trimmed = p.trim();
127:     const comparisonKey = normalizeWorkflowTriggerKey(trimmed);
128:     if (trimmed.includes('/') || trimmed.includes('\\')) return false;
129:     if (manualPhraseKeys.has(comparisonKey)) {
130:       return true;
131:     }
132:     if (/^\d{1,3}\s/.test(trimmed)) {
133:       return false;
134:     }
135:     return true;
136:   });
137: 
138:   // Stage 2: Remove entries where every word is under 3 characters (unless in allow-list)
139:   filtered = filtered.filter(p => {
140:     const comparisonKey = normalizeWorkflowTriggerKey(p);
141:     if (manualPhraseKeys.has(comparisonKey)) {
142:       return true;
143:     }
144:     const words = p.trim().split(/\s+/);
145:     if (words.length === 1 && words[0].length < 3 && !TRIGGER_ALLOW_LIST.has(words[0].toLowerCase())) {
146:       return false;
147:     }
148:     // Multi-word: keep if at least one word >= 3 chars or any word is in allow-list
149:     if (words.every(w => w.length < 3) && !words.some(w => TRIGGER_ALLOW_LIST.has(w.toLowerCase()))) {
150:       return false;
151:     }
152:     return true;
153:   });
154: 
155:   // Stage 3: Remove n-gram shingle phrases that are substrings of longer retained phrases
156:   const lowerPhrases = filtered.map((p) => p.toLowerCase().replace(/[-_]+/g, ' ').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim());
157:   filtered = filtered.filter((p, idx) => {
158:     const lower = lowerPhrases[idx];
159:     if (manualPhraseKeys.has(lower)) {
160:       return true;
161:     }
162:     if (isAllowlistedShortProductName(p)) {
163:       return true;
164:     }
165:     // Check if this phrase is a substring of any other (longer) phrase
166:     for (let j = 0; j < lowerPhrases.length; j++) {
167:       if (j !== idx && (
168:         (lowerPhrases[j] === lower && j < idx)
169:         || (lowerPhrases[j].length > lower.length && lowerPhrases[j].includes(lower))
170:       )) {
171:         return false;
172:       }
173:     }
174:     return true;
175:   });
176: 
177:   return filtered;
178: }
179: 
180: /**
181:  * Insert content after YAML frontmatter, preserving frontmatter at byte 0.
182:  * Frontmatter is a block delimited by `---\n` at position 0 and a closing `---\n`.
183:  * If no frontmatter is found, prepends the content (original behavior).
184:  */
185: function insertAfterFrontmatter(content: string, insertion: string): string {
186:   if (!content.startsWith('---')) {
187:     return insertion + content;
188:   }
189:   // Find the closing --- (skip the opening ---)
190:   const closingIdx = content.indexOf('\n---', 3);
191:   if (closingIdx === -1) {
192:     return insertion + content;
193:   }
194:   // Find the end of the closing --- line
195:   const afterClosing = content.indexOf('\n', closingIdx + 4);
196:   const insertionPoint = afterClosing === -1 ? content.length : afterClosing + 1;
197:   return content.slice(0, insertionPoint) + insertion + content.slice(insertionPoint);
198: }

235:   }

240:   if (typeof value === 'string') {
241:     return scrubWorkflowStringField(value, fieldPath, warn);

243: 
244:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {

246:   }
247: 
248:   if (value && typeof value === 'object') {
249:     return Object.fromEntries(
250:       Object.entries(value).map(([key, entry]) => [
251:         key,
252:         scrubWorkflowTextTree(entry, `${fieldPath}.${key}`, warn),
253:       ]),
254:     );
255:   }
256: 
257:   return value;

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SPECKIT: CHECK PREREQUISITES
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Validates spec folder exists with required files before implementation.

set -euo pipefail

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. ARGUMENT PARSING
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

JSON_MODE=false
REQUIRE_TASKS=false
INCLUDE_TASKS=false
PATHS_ONLY=false
VALIDATE_MODE=false
VALIDATE_STRICT=false
VALIDATE_VERBOSE=false

for arg in "$@"; do
    case "$arg" in
        --json) JSON_MODE=true ;;
        --require-tasks) REQUIRE_TASKS=true ;;
        --include-tasks) INCLUDE_TASKS=true ;;
        --paths-only) PATHS_ONLY=true ;;
        --validate|-V) VALIDATE_MODE=true ;;
        --validate-strict) VALIDATE_MODE=true; VALIDATE_STRICT=true ;;
        --validate-verbose) VALIDATE_MODE=true; VALIDATE_VERBOSE=true ;;
        --help|-h)
            cat << 'EOF'
check-prerequisites.sh - Spec folder prerequisite validation

USAGE: ./check-prerequisites.sh [OPTIONS]

OPTIONS:
  --json              Output in JSON format
  --require-tasks     Require tasks.md (for implementation phase)
  --include-tasks     Include tasks.md in AVAILABLE_DOCS
  --paths-only        Output paths only (no validation)
  --validate, -V      Run validate-spec.sh on folder
  --validate-strict   Strict mode (warnings as errors)
  --validate-verbose  Verbose validation output
  --help, -h          Show help
EOF
            exit 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown option '$arg'. Use --help for usage." >&2
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. INITIALIZATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "$SCRIPT_DIR/../common.sh"

# JSON escape helper for safe machine-readable output.
json_escape() {
    local str="$1"
    str="${str//\\/\\\\}"
    str="${str//\"/\\\"}"
    str="${str//$'\n'/\\n}"
    str="${str//$'\r'/\\r}"
    str="${str//$'\t'/\\t}"
    printf '%s' "$str"
}

# SECURITY: get_feature_paths uses printf %q for shell-safe output
eval "$(get_feature_paths)"
check_feature_branch "$CURRENT_BRANCH" "$HAS_GIT" || exit 1

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3. PATH-ONLY MODE
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

if $PATHS_ONLY; then
    if $JSON_MODE; then
        # Minimal JSON paths payload (no validation performed)
        printf '{"REPO_ROOT":"%s","BRANCH":"%s","FEATURE_DIR":"%s","FEATURE_SPEC":"%s","IMPL_PLAN":"%s","TASKS":"%s","RESEARCH_DIR":"%s","RESEARCH":"%s"}\n' \
            "$(json_escape "$REPO_ROOT")" \
            "$(json_escape "$CURRENT_BRANCH")" \
            "$(json_escape "$FEATURE_DIR")" \
            "$(json_escape "$FEATURE_SPEC")" \
            "$(json_escape "$IMPL_PLAN")" \
            "$(json_escape "$TASKS")" \
            "$(json_escape "$RESEARCH_DIR")" \
            "$(json_escape "$RESEARCH")"
    else
        echo "REPO_ROOT: $REPO_ROOT"
        echo "BRANCH: $CURRENT_BRANCH"
        echo "FEATURE_DIR: $FEATURE_DIR"
        echo "FEATURE_SPEC: $FEATURE_SPEC"
        echo "IMPL_PLAN: $IMPL_PLAN"
        echo "TASKS: $TASKS"
        echo "RESEARCH_DIR: $RESEARCH_DIR"
        echo "RESEARCH: $RESEARCH"
    fi
    exit 0
fi

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 4. VALIDATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

if [[ ! -d "$FEATURE_DIR" ]]; then
    if [[ "$CURRENT_BRANCH" =~ ^(main|master|trunk)$ ]]; then
        if $JSON_MODE; then
            printf '{"FEATURE_DIR":"","AVAILABLE_DOCS":[],"VALIDATION":"skipped","REASON":"%s"}\n' \
                "$(json_escape "No active feature spec folder for branch $CURRENT_BRANCH")"
        else
            echo "FEATURE_DIR:"
            echo "AVAILABLE_DOCS:"
            echo "VALIDATION: skipped (no active feature spec folder for branch $CURRENT_BRANCH)"
        fi
        exit 0

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3: # COMPONENT: SPEC KIT MEMORY MCP INSTALLER
4: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5: # Install and configure the Spec Kit Memory MCP Server.
6: # Provides semantic vector search for conversation context,
7: # decisions, and session memories. Enables context preservation
8: # across sessions with constitutional tier priorities.
9: 
10: set -euo pipefail
11: 
12: readonly SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
13: source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_utils.sh"
14: 
15: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16: # 1. CONFIGURATION
17: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18: readonly MCP_NAME="mk-spec-memory"
19: readonly MCP_DISPLAY_NAME="Spec Kit Memory"
20: readonly SPEC_KIT_ROOT_DIR=".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit"
21: readonly MCP_SERVER_DIR="${SPEC_KIT_ROOT_DIR}/mcp_server"
22: readonly MCP_SERVER_SCRIPT="dist/context-server.js"
23: readonly MCP_CANONICAL_DB_DIR="${MCP_SERVER_DIR}/database"
24: readonly MIN_NODE_VERSION="20.11.0"
25: SKIP_VERIFY="${SKIP_VERIFY:-false}"
26: 
27: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28: # 2. FUNCTIONS
29: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30: 
31: install_mcp() {
32:     log_step "Checking prerequisites..."
33: 
34:     # Log Node.js environment for diagnostics
35:     log_info "Node.js version: $(node --version)"
36:     log_info "Node.js MODULE_VERSION: $(node -e 'console.log(process.versions.modules)')"
37:     log_info "Node.js path: $(which node)"
38: 
39:     local project_root
40:     project_root=$(get_project_root) || exit 1
41:     local spec_root="${project_root}/${SPEC_KIT_ROOT_DIR}"
42:     local server_dir="${project_root}/${MCP_SERVER_DIR}"
43:     local server_script_path="${server_dir}/${MCP_SERVER_SCRIPT}"
44: 
45:     # Check if spec-kit root exists
46:     if [[ ! -d "${spec_root}" ]]; then
47:         log_error "Spec Kit root directory not found: ${spec_root}"
48:         log_info "Ensure .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit exists in your project."
49:         exit 1
50:     fi
51: 
52:     # Check if server directory exists
53:     if [[ ! -d "${server_dir}" ]]; then
54:         log_error "Spec Kit Memory server directory not found: ${server_dir}"
55:         log_info "This MCP is bundled with the project. Ensure .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit exists."
56:         exit 1
57:     fi
58: 
59:     # Check if workspace package.json exists
60:     if [[ ! -f "${spec_root}/package.json" ]]; then
61:         log_error "package.json not found in ${spec_root}"
62:         exit 1
63:     fi
64: 
65:     # Check if package.json exists
66:     if [[ ! -f "${server_dir}/package.json" ]]; then
67:         log_error "package.json not found in ${server_dir}"
68:         exit 1
69:     fi
70: 
71:     # Clear stale HuggingFace model cache to prevent version mismatches
72:     local hf_cache_dir="${server_dir}/node_modules/@huggingface/transformers/.cache"
73:     if [[ -d "${hf_cache_dir}" ]]; then
74:         log_info "Clearing stale HuggingFace model cache..."
75:         rm -rf "${hf_cache_dir}" 2>/dev/null || true
76:     fi
77: 
78:     # Remove native modules before install to prevent ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED
79:     log_info "Removing stale native modules..."
80:     for mod_dir in "${server_dir}/node_modules/better-sqlite3" "${server_dir}/node_modules/sqlite-vec"; do
81:         if [[ -d "${mod_dir}" ]]; then
82:             rm -rf "${mod_dir}" 2>/dev/null || true
83:         fi
84:     done
85: 
86:     # Install dependencies from workspace root (covers shared + mcp_server + scripts)
87:     log_step "Installing dependencies from spec-kit root..."
88:     (
89:         cd "${spec_root}"
90:         npm install --silent 2>/dev/null || npm install
91:     )
92: 
93:     if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
94:         log_success "Dependencies installed successfully"
95:     else
96:         log_error "Failed to install dependencies"
97:         exit 1
98:     fi
99: 
100:     # Build TypeScript from workspace root; if strict build fails, use fallback

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: SPEC KIT MEMORY MCP INSTALLER
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Install and configure the Spec Kit Memory MCP Server.
# Provides semantic vector search for conversation context,
# decisions, and session memories. Enables context preservation
# across sessions with constitutional tier priorities.

set -euo pipefail

readonly SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_utils.sh"

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. CONFIGURATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
readonly MCP_NAME="mk-spec-memory"
readonly MCP_DISPLAY_NAME="Spec Kit Memory"
readonly SPEC_KIT_ROOT_DIR=".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit"
readonly MCP_SERVER_DIR="${SPEC_KIT_ROOT_DIR}/mcp_server"
readonly MCP_SERVER_SCRIPT="dist/context-server.js"
readonly MCP_CANONICAL_DB_DIR="${MCP_SERVER_DIR}/database"
readonly MIN_NODE_VERSION="20.11.0"
SKIP_VERIFY="${SKIP_VERIFY:-false}"

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. FUNCTIONS
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

install_mcp() {
    log_step "Checking prerequisites..."

    # Log Node.js environment for diagnostics
    log_info "Node.js version: $(node --version)"
    log_info "Node.js MODULE_VERSION: $(node -e 'console.log(process.versions.modules)')"
    log_info "Node.js path: $(which node)"

    local project_root
    project_root=$(get_project_root) || exit 1
    local spec_root="${project_root}/${SPEC_KIT_ROOT_DIR}"
    local server_dir="${project_root}/${MCP_SERVER_DIR}"
    local server_script_path="${server_dir}/${MCP_SERVER_SCRIPT}"

    # Check if spec-kit root exists
    if [[ ! -d "${spec_root}" ]]; then
        log_error "Spec Kit root directory not found: ${spec_root}"
        log_info "Ensure .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit exists in your project."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check if server directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "${server_dir}" ]]; then
        log_error "Spec Kit Memory server directory not found: ${server_dir}"
        log_info "This MCP is bundled with the project. Ensure .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit exists."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check if workspace package.json exists
    if [[ ! -f "${spec_root}/package.json" ]]; then
        log_error "package.json not found in ${spec_root}"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check if package.json exists
    if [[ ! -f "${server_dir}/package.json" ]]; then
        log_error "package.json not found in ${server_dir}"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Clear stale HuggingFace model cache to prevent version mismatches
    local hf_cache_dir="${server_dir}/node_modules/@huggingface/transformers/.cache"
    if [[ -d "${hf_cache_dir}" ]]; then
        log_info "Clearing stale HuggingFace model cache..."
        rm -rf "${hf_cache_dir}" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    # Remove native modules before install to prevent ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED
    log_info "Removing stale native modules..."
    for mod_dir in "${server_dir}/node_modules/better-sqlite3" "${server_dir}/node_modules/sqlite-vec"; do
        if [[ -d "${mod_dir}" ]]; then
            rm -rf "${mod_dir}" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    done

    # Install dependencies from workspace root (covers shared + mcp_server + scripts)
    log_step "Installing dependencies from spec-kit root..."
    (
        cd "${spec_root}"
        npm install --silent 2>/dev/null || npm install
    )

    if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "Dependencies installed successfully"
    else
        log_error "Failed to install dependencies"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Build TypeScript from workspace root; if strict build fails, use fallback
    log_step "Building TypeScript workspace..."
    if (
        cd "${spec_root}"
        npm run build
    ); then
        log_success "TypeScript build completed via npm run build"
    else
        log_warn "npm run build failed - retrying with fallback: npx tsc --build --noCheck --force"
        (
            cd "${spec_root}"
            npx tsc --build --noCheck --force
        )
        log_success "TypeScript build completed via fallback"
    fi

    # Check native modules and auto-rebuild if probe reports failures
    log_step "Checking native module health..."
    local native_check_output
    native_check_output="$(
        cd "${spec_root}"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Shared helpers for Spec Kit setup installers.

set -euo pipefail

if [[ -t 1 ]]; then
    RED='\033[0;31m'
    GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
    BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    NC='\033[0m'
else
    RED=''
    GREEN=''
    YELLOW=''
    BLUE=''
    CYAN=''
    NC=''
fi
readonly RED GREEN YELLOW BLUE CYAN NC

log_info() { printf '%b[INFO]%b %s\n' "$BLUE" "$NC" "$1"; }
log_success() { printf '%b[OK]%b %s\n' "$GREEN" "$NC" "$1"; }
log_warn() { printf '%b[WARN]%b %s\n' "$YELLOW" "$NC" "$1"; }
log_error() { printf '%b[ERROR]%b %s\n' "$RED" "$NC" "$1" >&2; }
log_step() { printf '%b[STEP]%b %s\n' "$CYAN" "$NC" "$1"; }

check_command() {
    command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1
}

check_node_version() {
    local min_version="${1:-20.11.0}"
    if ! check_command node; then
        log_error "Node.js is not installed"
        return 1
    fi

    local actual
    actual="$(node --version | sed 's/^v//')"
    if ! node -e '
const actual = process.argv[1].split(".").map(Number);
const min = process.argv[2].split(".").map(Number);
for (let i = 0; i < 3; i += 1) {
  if ((actual[i] || 0) > (min[i] || 0)) process.exit(0);
  if ((actual[i] || 0) < (min[i] || 0)) process.exit(1);
}
process.exit(0);
' "$actual" "$min_version"; then
        log_error "Node.js version ${min_version}+ required (found: v${actual})"
        return 1
    fi

    log_success "Node.js v${actual} detected"
}

check_npm() {
    if ! check_command npm; then
        log_error "npm is not installed"
        return 1
    fi
    log_success "npm v$(npm --version) detected"
}

check_npx() {
    if ! check_command npx; then
        log_error "npx is not available"
        return 1
    fi
    log_success "npx is available"
}

get_project_root() {
    if git rev-parse --show-toplevel >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        git rev-parse --show-toplevel
        return 0
    fi

    local dir="$PWD"
    while [[ "$dir" != "/" ]]; do
        if [[ -f "$dir/opencode.json" || -d "$dir/.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit" ]]; then
            printf '%s\n' "$dir"
            return 0
        fi
        dir="$(dirname "$dir")"
    done

    log_error "Unable to locate project root"
    return 1
}

json_validate() {
    local file="$1"
    if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
        log_error "File not found: $file"
        return 1
    fi

    python3 - "$file" <<'PY'
import json
import sys
with open(sys.argv[1], "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
    json.load(fh)
PY
}

json_has_key() {
    local file="$1"
    local dotted_path="$2"
    [[ -f "$file" ]] || return 1

    python3 - "$file" "$dotted_path" <<'PY'
import json
import sys
with open(sys.argv[1], "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
    data = json.load(fh)
current = data
for key in sys.argv[2].lstrip(".").split("."):
    if not isinstance(current, dict) or key not in current:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: Rebuild Native Modules
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Rebuilds native Node.js modules after a Node version change.
# Run from spec-kit root: bash scripts/setup/rebuild-native-modules.sh
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"

echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Rebuilding Native Modules                               ║"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""
echo "Node.js:        $(node --version)"
echo "MODULE_VERSION: $(node -e 'console.log(process.versions.modules)')"
echo "Platform:       $(uname -s) $(uname -m)"
echo ""

# Step 1: Rebuild better-sqlite3 (mcp_server)
echo "── Step 1: Rebuilding better-sqlite3..."
cd "$ROOT_DIR/mcp_server"
npm rebuild better-sqlite3
echo "   ✓ better-sqlite3 rebuilt"

# Step 2: Rebuild shared workspace native modules
echo "── Step 2: Rebuilding shared workspace modules..."
cd "$ROOT_DIR/shared"
if [[ -d "node_modules" ]]; then
  npm rebuild 2>/dev/null || echo "   (no native modules in shared/)"
else
  echo "   (shared/node_modules not found — run npm install first)" >&2
fi
echo "   ✓ shared modules rebuilt"

# Step 3: Optionally clear HuggingFace cache
HF_CACHE="$HOME/.cache/huggingface/hub"
if [[ -d "$HF_CACHE" ]]; then
  echo ""
  echo "── Step 3: HuggingFace cache found at $HF_CACHE"
  read -p "   Clear HuggingFace cache? (y/N): " -n 1 -r
  echo ""
  if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    rm -rf "$HF_CACHE"
    echo "   ✓ HuggingFace cache cleared"
  else
    echo "   ⊘ Skipped"
  fi
fi

# Step 4: Write .node-version-marker
echo ""
echo "── Step 4: Writing .node-version-marker..."
node "$ROOT_DIR/scripts/setup/record-node-version.js"
echo "   ✓ .node-version-marker updated"

echo ""
echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║  Done! Restart the MCP server to apply changes.         ║"
echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"

# Exit codes:
#   0 - Success
